meta.setting: Knife-edge ridge trails, shale ledges, switchbacks, pack strings, and storm exposure above the mining roads
meta.rdr_clone_target: RDR-clone western with dangerous travel, animal handling, ambush lines, and weather exposure

research_grounding.title: NPS Whiskeytown History and Culture
research_grounding.use: Rugged terrain, roads, mining travel, logging, and settlement pressure around Whiskeytown.

research_grounding.title: California Department of Fish and Wildlife Mountain Lion
research_grounding.use: Cougar presence and sign in California rough country.

generation_prompt.task: Generate the Knife-Edge Trails region page for the 1899 Jefferson State western packet. This JSON contains shape and guidance only.
page_shape: H3 local marker line in the register of Loose Shale, Pack Rope, and Long Drop
square image reference immediately after the marker line
opening blockquote built as three short generated lines: trail object, wind or animal sign, plain table meaning
two compact paragraphs establishing ridge trails as travel hazard, lookout line, ambush approach, pack-animal test, and weather clock
closing blockquote that gives a generated trail sign and practical table meaning
generation_guidance: Generate knife-edge trails as narrow practical routes used because the long road costs too much time: shale, scrub oak, mule bells, switchback dust, snapped cinches, and a drop that keeps everyone honest.
Give the table hooks around a stalled pack string, cougar tracks in dust, a rider forced to lead his horse, a loose saddle load, shots echoing wrong, or storm clouds cutting off retreat.
Make movement matter; the page should guide who can pass, who must dismount, what falls, what can be heard, and where cover exists.
Use trail-craft, fear, and stubborn necessity rather than grand scenery.
generation_prompt.hand_cut_note: The trail should feel whittled into the hill by hooves that never liked it.
generation_prompt.table_spark: A knife-edge trail scene should ask what is too heavy, who is blocking the line, and what sound came from above or below.
must_preserve: Use 1899 western trail, pack-animal, and weather language only.
The generated page must include image path images/knife-edge-trails.jpg.
The prompt must not contain final prose, final blockquotes, or finished field marks.
avoid: do not mention any prior packet, inherited taxonomy, or non-western source language
do not describe travel as glamorous or painless
do not use modern hiking gear, railings, paved paths, bright trail blazes, or resort overlook language
self_check: The future markdown follows the exact heading sequence.
The future page is about ridge travel as hazard, route choice, and scene engine.
The prompt contains no finished page prose.
All language belongs to grounded 1899 Jefferson State western play.
